devanagari: अथ सप्तमोऽध्यायः । श्रीराजोवाच । येन येनावतारेण भगवान्हरिरीश्वरः । करोति कर्णरम्याणि मनोज्ञानि च नः प्रभो ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: atha saptamo'dhyāyaḥśrīrājovācayena yenāvatāreṇa bhagavānharirīśvaraḥkaroti karṇaramyāṇi manojñāni ca naḥ prabho
translation:
The King (Parīkṣit) urged:
whatever (sportive) acts the glorious Lord Hari, the Supreme Ruler, does in whatever incarnation (like the Fish, the Tortoise etc. the accounts of which are narrated by you) they are pleasing to the ear and fascinating to our minds, Oh Master.

devanagari: यच्छृण्वतोऽपैत्यरतिर्वितृष्णा सत्त्वं च शुद्ध्यत्यचिरेण पुंसः । भक्तिर्हरौ तत्पुरुषे च सख्यं तदेव हारं वद मन्यसे चेत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yacchṛṇvato'paityaratirvitṛṣṇā sattvaṃ ca śuddhyatyacireṇa puṃsaḥbhaktirharau tatpuruṣe ca sakhyaṃ tadeva hāraṃ vada manyase cet
translation: May it please you to favour us by narrating that very fascinating account (of Lord Hari’s deeds), by listening to which (the feeling of I despondency and the various cravings (which generate it) fade away (leaving no trace behind), the mind gets purified in a short time, and devotion unto Lord Hari and friendly feelings towards his votaries are engendered.

devanagari: अथान्यदपि कृष्णस्य तोकाचरितमद्भुतम् । मानुषं लोकमासाद्य तज्जातिमनुरुन्धतः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: athānyadapi kṛṣṇasya tokācaritamadbhutammānuṣaṃ lokamāsādya tajjātimanurundhataḥ
translation:
Do you describe to us any other marvellous deed of Kṛṣṇa as a child who, after his descent in the human (form and) world, enacted (imitated) the ways of human beings.
[Padaratnāvalī’s text and Vṛndāvana edition:]
3(A). Oh Master! You are gracious unto us. So out of mercy towards me, be pleased to describe in details (Kṛṣṇa’s sportive acts in childhood) which (completely) annihilate all sins, Oh past-master in Dharma!

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । कदाचिदौत्थानिककौतुकाप्लवे जन्मर्क्षयोगे समवेतयोषिताम् । वादित्रगीतद्विजमन्त्रवाचकैश्चकार सूनोरभिषेचनं सती ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācakadācidautthānikakautukāplave janmarkṣayoge samavetayoṣitāmvāditragītadvijamantravācakaiścakāra sūnorabhiṣecanaṃ satī
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
One day (at the end of the third month), when the constellation (Rohiṇī) that presided at the birth of Kṛṣṇa was in ascendance, and coincided with the ceremonial festive ablution to celebrate the infant’s (Kṛṣṇa’s) turning in the bed was to take place, the chaste lady Yaśodā performed the rite of auspicious bathing of the child in the assemblage of Gopa women (who visited her house for this occasion), while musical instruments were played, songs were sung and benedictory Mantras were being chanted (by Brāhmaṇas).

devanagari: नन्दस्य पत्नी कृतमज्जनादिकं विप्रैः कृतस्वस्त्ययनं सुपूजितैः । अन्नाद्यवासःस्रगभीष्टधेनुभिः सञ्जातनिद्रा क्षमशीशयच्छनैः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: nandasya patnī kṛtamajjanādikaṃ vipraiḥ kṛtasvastyayanaṃ supūjitaiḥannādyavāsaḥsragabhīṣṭadhenubhiḥ sañjātanidrā kṣamaśīśayacchanaiḥ
translation: When the infant’s bath and toilet were over, and Brāhmaṇas who were duly honoured with liberal gifts of (sumptuous) food, garments, garlands and cows of their choice, pronounced the auspicious benediction (with Vedic Mantras), Yośodā (the generous wife of liberal Nanda) seeing her child’s eyes closing in sleep, gently put him to bed (in a cradle under a cart).

devanagari: औत्थानिकौत्सुक्यमना मनस्विनी समागतान्पूजयती व्रजौकसः । नैवाशृणोद्वै रुदितं सुतस्य सा रुदन्स्तनार्थी चरणावुदक्षिपत् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: autthānikautsukyamanā manasvinī samāgatānpūjayatī vrajaukasaḥnaivāśṛṇodvai ruditaṃ sutasya sā rudanstanārthī caraṇāvudakṣipat
translation: With her mind full of enthusiasm on the festive occasion of Krṣṇa’s turning in bed, the ṇoble-minded lady, who was busy in receiving with honour the inhābitants of Vraja who came to (congratulate her) on that occasion, did not hear the crying of her child who, wishing to suck his mother, kicked up his tiny feet.

devanagari: अधःशयानस्य शिशोरनोऽल्पक प्रवालमृद्वङ्घ्रिहतं व्यवर्तत । विध्वस्तनानारसकुप्यभाजनं व्यत्यस्तचक्राक्षविभिन्नकूबरम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: adhaḥśayānasya śiśorano'lpaka pravālamṛdvaṅghrihataṃ vyavartatavidhvastanānārasakupyabhājanaṃ vyatyastacakrākṣavibhinnakūbaram
translation: Being hit by the small feet, delicate like tender shoots of the baby that was lying beneath, the cart was overturned with its upside down, and all the pots of bell-metal containing various liquid delicacies (and other sweets) were crushed and the wheels, the axle tree were overturned and its yoke shattered.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा यशोदाप्रमुखा व्रजस्त्रिय । औत्थानिके कर्मणि याः समागताः । नन्दादयश्चाद्भुतदर्शनाकुलाः । कथं स्वयं वै शकटं विपर्यगात् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā yaśodāpramukhā vrajastriyaautthānike karmaṇi yāḥ samāgatāḥnandādayaścādbhutadarśanākulāḥkathaṃ svayaṃ vai śakaṭaṃ viparyagāt
translation:
Noticing it (the overturned position of the cart) Yaśodā and other women of Vraja who assembled there for the festive occasion (of Kṛṣṇa’s turning in the bed) and Nanda and other cowherds as well, were perplexed at that marvellous sight and wondered, “How this cart could automatically be overturned?”
[Bhāgavata Candrikā and Padaratnāvalī’s text and Vṛndāvana edition:]
8(A). Speaking thus and arguing about its cause in a perplexed manner, the people of Vraja surrounded the cart in a distressed mood, as it were.

devanagari: ऊचुरव्यवसितमतीन्गोपान्गोपीश्च बालकाः । रुदतानेन पादेन क्षिप्तमेतन्न संशयः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: ūcuravyavasitamatīngopāngopīśca bālakāḥrudatānena pādena kṣiptametanna saṃśayaḥ
translation:
Childern (playing nearby) told the Gopas and their wives whose minds were confused and uncertain (about the cause of overturning of the cart)—
“There is absolutely no doubt that it was upturned with the foot (kick) by this (Kṛṣṇa) while crying”.

devanagari: न ते श्रद्दधिरे गोपा बालभाषितमित्युत । अप्रमेयं बलं तस्य बालकस्य न ते विदुः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: na te śraddadhire gopā bālabhāṣitamityutaaprameyaṃ balaṃ tasya bālakasya na te viduḥ
translation: But the Gopas disbelieved it as mere prattle of children, as they did not know the immeasurable might of that baby.

devanagari: रुदन्तं सुतमादाय यशोदा ग्रहशङ्किता । कृतस्वस्त्ययनं विप्रैः सूक्तैः स्तनमपाययत् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: rudantaṃ sutamādāya yaśodā grahaśaṅkitākṛtasvastyayanaṃ vipraiḥ sūktaiḥ stanamapāyayat
translation: Taking up her son, Yaśodā who suspected the trouble of evil spirits, made Brāhmaṇas to recite the benedictory Vedic hymns (to drive out the spirits), and to bless the child, and gave him suck.

devanagari: पूर्ववत्स्थापितं गोपैर्बलिभिः सपरिच्छदम् । विप्रा हुत्वार्चयां चक्रुर्दध्यक्षतकुशाम्बुभिः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: pūrvavatsthāpitaṃ gopairbalibhiḥ saparicchadamviprā hutvārcayāṃ cakrurdadhyakṣatakuśāmbubhiḥ
translation: Brāhmaṇas offered oblations to fire (to propitiate the presiding deities of planets).[1] When powerful Gopas assembled (the parts of) the cart as before along with the metal pots of dairy products etc., they worshipped it with curds, unbroken rice-grains, Kuśa grass and water[2] (as that was the symbol of their calling and the abode of Lakṣmī).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Or: the offerings of bali (cooked rice) were distributed to eight directions—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

Footnote [2]:
Or: with unbroken rice mixed with curds and sprinkling water with Kuśa grass (VT.)

devanagari: येऽसूयानृतदम्भेर्षा हिंसामानविवर्जिताः । न तेषां सत्यशीलानामाशिषो विफलाः कृताः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: ye'sūyānṛtadambherṣā hiṃsāmānavivarjitāḥna teṣāṃ satyaśīlānāmāśiṣo viphalāḥ kṛtāḥ
translation: “The benedictions uttered by those who are truthful by nature and completely free from jealousy, falsehood, hypocrisy, intolerance, violence and pride, never become fruitless.”

devanagari: इति बालकमादाय सामर्ग्यजुरुपाकृतैः । जलैः पवित्रौषधिभिरभिषिच्य द्विजोत्तमैः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti bālakamādāya sāmargyajurupākṛtaiḥjalaiḥ pavitrauṣadhibhirabhiṣicya dvijottamaiḥ
translation: With this conviction, the cowherd Nanda took up the child and got it sprinkled with water sanctified with sacred mantras from the Sāma, Ṛg and Yajur-Vedas, by the best of Brāhmaṇas. Making the Brāhmaṇas pronounce the prescribed benedictory hymns (Svastyayana) and offering oblations to fire, Nanda with a serene mind, gave them a heavy feast (of rich, delicious articles of food).

devanagari: वाचयित्वा स्वस्त्ययनं नन्दगोपः समाहितः । हुत्वा चाग्निं द्विजातिभ्यः प्रादादन्नं महागुणम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: vācayitvā svastyayanaṃ nandagopaḥ samāhitaḥhutvā cāgniṃ dvijātibhyaḥ prādādannaṃ mahāguṇam
translation: With this conviction, the cowherd Nanda took up the child and got it sprinkled with water sanctified with sacred mantras from the Sāma, Ṛg and Yajur-Vedas, by the best of Brāhmaṇas. Making the Brāhmaṇas pronounce the prescribed benedictory hymns (Svastyayana) and offering oblations to fire, Nanda with a serene mind, gave them a heavy feast (of rich, delicious articles of food).

devanagari: गावः सर्वगुणोपेता वासःस्रग्रुक्ममालिनीः । आत्मजाभ्युदयार्थाय प्रादात्ते चान्वयुञ्जत ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: gāvaḥ sarvaguṇopetā vāsaḥsragrukmamālinīḥātmajābhyudayārthāya prādātte cānvayuñjata
translation: For the all-round prosperity of the child, Nanda donated to them cows possessing all good (and auspicious) qualities, adorned them with cloth, garlands and necklaces of gold coins. And Brāhmaṇas, on their part, pronounced blessings to the child.

devanagari: विप्रा मन्त्रविदो युक्तास्तैर्याः प्रोक्तास्तथाशिषः । ता निष्फला भविष्यन्ति न कदाचिदपि स्फुटम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: viprā mantravido yuktāstairyāḥ proktāstathāśiṣaḥtā niṣphalā bhaviṣyanti na kadācidapi sphuṭam
translation: It is obvious that whatever benedictions are pronounced by Brāhmaṇas who are well-versed in Vedas and devoted to God shall never turn out to be fruitless.

devanagari: एकदारोहमारूढं लालयन्ती सुतं सती । गरिमाणं शिशोर्वोढुं न सेहे गिरिकूटवत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadārohamārūḍhaṃ lālayantī sutaṃ satīgarimāṇaṃ śiśorvoḍhuṃ na sehe girikūṭavat
translation: On one day, the pious lady seated the child in her lap and was fondling him. But (suddenly) she was not able to bear the weight of the Baby, as if it was heavy like a mountain peak.

devanagari: भूमौ निधाय तं गोपी विस्मिता भारपीडिता । महापुरुषमादध्यौ जगतामास कर्मसु ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūmau nidhāya taṃ gopī vismitā bhārapīḍitāmahāpuruṣamādadhyau jagatāmāsa karmasu
translation: The cowherdess being oppressed with the burden (of the child), was astonished and placed him on the ground. She contemplated on the Supreme Lord not knowing that the whole universe is contained in his body and set about her work.

devanagari: दैत्यो नाम्ना तृणावर्तः कंसभृत्यः प्रणोदितः । चक्रवातस्वरूपेण जहारासीनमर्भकम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: daityo nāmnā tṛṇāvartaḥ kaṃsabhṛtyaḥ praṇoditaḥcakravātasvarūpeṇa jahārāsīnamarbhakam
translation: A demon called Tṛṇāvarta in the service of Kaṃsa was specially detailed and deputed (to kill Kṛṣṇa). Assuming the form of a whirl-wind, he carried away the child that was sitting.

devanagari: गोकुलं सर्वमावृण्वन्मुष्णंश्चक्षूंषि रेणुभिः । ईरयन्सुमहाघोर शब्देन प्रदिशो दिशः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: gokulaṃ sarvamāvṛṇvanmuṣṇaṃścakṣūṃṣi reṇubhiḥīrayansumahāghora śabdena pradiśo diśaḥ
translation: He enveloped the whole of Gokula with a cloud of dust and blinded the eyes (of the inhabitants). He made the quarters and intermediate points tremble with his extremely terrible roar.

devanagari: मुहूर्तमभवद्गोष्ठं रजसा तमसावृतम् । सुतं यशोदा नापश्यत्तस्मिन्न्यस्तवती यतः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: muhūrtamabhavadgoṣṭhaṃ rajasā tamasāvṛtamsutaṃ yaśodā nāpaśyattasminnyastavatī yataḥ
translation: For an hour or so the whole of Gokula was covered with (a cloud of) dust and darkness. Yaśodā could not see her son at the place where she kept him.

devanagari: नापश्यत्कश्चनात्मानं परं चापि विमोहितः । तृणावर्तनिसृष्टाभिः शर्कराभिरुपद्रुतः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: nāpaśyatkaścanātmānaṃ paraṃ cāpi vimohitaḥtṛṇāvartanisṛṣṭābhiḥ śarkarābhirupadrutaḥ
translation: Being assailed by sand particles wafted by Tṛṇāvarta, nobody could see himself or another person in that blinding confusion.

devanagari: इति खरपवनचक्रपांशुवर्षे सुतपदवीमबलाविलक्ष्य माता । अतिकरुमनुस्मरन्त्यशोचद्भुवि पतिता मृतवत्सका यथा गौः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti kharapavanacakrapāṃśuvarṣe sutapadavīmabalāvilakṣya mātāatikarumanusmarantyaśocadbhuvi patitā mṛtavatsakā yathā gauḥ
translation: Thus not being able to find the trace of her son in the heavy downpour of dust caused by the whirlwind of vehement velocity, the helpless mother (Yaśodā), brooding over him, tenderly began to bewail in the most pitiable manner, and dropped down to the ground like unto a cow bereaved of her calf.

devanagari: रुदितमनुनिशम्य तत्र गोप्यो भृशमनुतप्तधियोऽश्रुपूर्णमुख्यः । रुरुदुरनुपलभ्य नन्दसूनुं पवन उपारतपांशुवर्षवेगे ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ruditamanuniśamya tatra gopyo bhṛśamanutaptadhiyo'śrupūrṇamukhyaḥruruduranupalabhya nandasūnuṃ pavana upāratapāṃśuvarṣavege
translation: When the vehemence of the stormy wind and of the dust-shower abated, the Gopīs (cowherd-women) heard the lamentations of Yaśodā. Not finding Nanda’s son there, they were extremely tormented at heart, and broke out crying with their faces flooded with tears.

devanagari: तृणावर्तः शान्तरयो वात्यारूपधरो हरन् । कृष्णं नभोगतो गन्तुं नाशक्नोद्भूरिभारभृत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tṛṇāvartaḥ śāntarayo vātyārūpadharo harankṛṣṇaṃ nabhogato gantuṃ nāśaknodbhūribhārabhṛt
translation: Having assumed the form of a whirlwind, Tṛṇāvarta carried away Kṛṣṇa to the skies. But in consequence of his having been compelled to bear the (unbearably) stupendous weight of Kṛṣṇa, he could not proceed further (beyond a certain height) and his velocity abated (to a stand-still).

devanagari: तमश्मानं मन्यमान आत्मनो गुरुमत्तया । गले गृहीत उत्स्रष्टुं नाशक्नोदद्भुतार्भकम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tamaśmānaṃ manyamāna ātmano gurumattayāgale gṛhīta utsraṣṭuṃ nāśaknodadbhutārbhakam
translation: Due to his extraordinary weight, the Asura thought him to be a mountain. As he was tightly gripped at the throat by him, he could not disengage himself from that wonderful (superhuman) child.

devanagari: गलग्रहणनिश्चेष्टो दैत्यो निर्गतलोचनः । अव्यक्तरावो न्यपतत्सहबालो व्यसुर्व्रजे ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: galagrahaṇaniśceṣṭo daityo nirgatalocanaḥavyaktarāvo nyapatatsahabālo vyasurvraje
translation: Being tightly clasped at the throat with extraordinary pressure, he was rendered motionless. His eyes came out of their sockets. Withan indistinct groan he fell dead on the ground at Vraja, with the child dangling from his neck.

devanagari: तमन्तरिक्षात्पतितं शिलायां विशीर्णसर्वावयवं करालम् । पुरं यथा रुद्र शरेण विद्धं स्त्रियो रुदत्यो ददृशुः समेताः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tamantarikṣātpatitaṃ śilāyāṃ viśīrṇasarvāvayavaṃ karālampuraṃ yathā rudra śareṇa viddhaṃ striyo rudatyo dadṛśuḥ sametāḥ
translation: The bewailing women who assembled there saw the terrible demon falling from the sky on a rock, getting all his limbs shattered like the city (Tripura) cleft by the arrow of Rudra.

devanagari: प्रादाय मात्रे प्रतिहृत्य विस्मिताः कृष्णं च तस्योरसि लम्बमानम् । तं स्वस्तिमन्तं पुरुषादनीतं विहायसा मृत्युमुखात्प्रमुक्तम् । गोप्यश्च...
english_devnagari: prādāya mātre pratihṛtya vismitāḥ kṛṣṇaṃ ca tasyorasi lambamānamtaṃ svastimantaṃ puruṣādanītaṃ vihāyasā mṛtyumukhātpramuktamgopyaśca gopāḥ kila nandamukhyā labdhvā punaḥ prāpuratīva modam
translation: Astonished to see Kṛṣṇa dangling on the chest of the demon, they picked him up and restored him to his mother. Finding him safe and sound in body though carried away through the sky by the demon, and getting him back unscathed from the jaws of death, Gopīs and Gopas headed by Nanda were verily filled with rapturous delight.

devanagari: अहो बतात्यद्भुतमेष रक्षसा बालो निवृत्तिं गमितोऽभ्यगात्पुनः । हिंस्रः स्वपापेन विहिंसितः खलः साधुः समत्वेन भयाद्विमुच्यते ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: aho batātyadbhutameṣa rakṣasā bālo nivṛttiṃ gamito'bhyagātpunaḥhiṃsraḥ svapāpena vihiṃsitaḥ khalaḥ sādhuḥ samatvena bhayādvimucyate
translation: “Oh! How miraculous! This child, carried by the demon, and (practically) brought to its end (death), has come back safe! While the blood-thirsty wicked (demon) was killed under the weight of his own sins, and a pious soul is saved from fear due to his even-ness of attitude (towards all).

devanagari: किं नस्तपश्चीर्णमधोक्षजार्चनं । पूर्तेष्टदत्तमुत भूतसौहृदम् । यत्सम्परेतः पुनरेव बालको । दिष्ट्या स्वबन्धून्प्रणयन्नुपस्थितः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ nastapaścīrṇamadhokṣajārcanaṃpūrteṣṭadattamuta bhūtasauhṛdamyatsamparetaḥ punareva bālakodiṣṭyā svabandhūnpraṇayannupasthitaḥ
translation: Oh! What austere penance, what adoration to Lord Viṣṇu, what charitable acts in public interest (like building of temples or tanks), what sacrificial performances, what deeds benevolent to living beings have been done by us that (by virtue of which) the child which was as good as dead, has been fortunately restored to us, bringing delight to us, his kinsfolk.”

devanagari: दृष्ट्वाद्भुतानि बहुशो नन्दगोपो बृहद्वने । वसुदेववचो भूयो मानयामास विस्मितः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvādbhutāni bahuśo nandagopo bṛhadvanevasudevavaco bhūyo mānayāmāsa vismitaḥ
translation: Amazed at the frequent recurrence of such miracles in that big forest (Gokula), Nanda highly esteemed the (parting) words of Vasudeva.

devanagari: एकदार्भकमादाय स्वाङ्कमारोप्य भामिनी । प्रस्नुतं पाययामास स्तनं स्नेहपरिप्लुता ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadārbhakamādāya svāṅkamāropya bhāminīprasnutaṃ pāyayāmāsa stanaṃ snehapariplutā
translation: One day the beautiful lady (Yaśodā) placed the child on her lap, and being overcome with motherly affection made him suck her breast overflowing with milk.

devanagari: पीतप्रायस्य जननी सुतस्य रुचिरस्मितम् । मुखं लालयती राजञ्जृम्भतो ददृशे इदम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: pītaprāyasya jananī sutasya rucirasmitammukhaṃ lālayatī rājañjṛmbhato dadṛśe idam
translation: When his sucking was almost over, she was caressing his face which was beaming with graceful smile. And Lo! Oh King! When he yawned she saw the following in his mouth:

devanagari: खं रोदसी ज्योतिरनीकमाशाः सूर्येन्दुवह्निश्वसनाम्बुधींश्च । द्वीपान्नगांस्तद्दुहितॄर्वनानि भूतानि यानि स्थिरजङ्गमानि ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: khaṃ rodasī jyotiranīkamāśāḥ sūryenduvahniśvasanāmbudhīṃścadvīpānnagāṃstadduhitṝrvanāni bhūtāni yāni sthirajaṅgamāni
translation:
When his sucking was almost over, she was caressing his face which was beaming with graceful smile. And Lo! Oh King! When he yawned she saw the following in his mouth:
The sky, the firmament above and the earth below, host of luminaries, the cardinal points, the Sun, the Moon, the firegod, the wind-god, oceans, continents, mountains and their daughters (i.e. rivers), forests and the mobile and immobile creation.

devanagari: सा वीक्ष्य विश्वं सहसा राजन्सञ्जातवेपथुः । सम्मील्य मृगशावाक्षी नेत्रे आसीत्सुविस्मिता ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: sā vīkṣya viśvaṃ sahasā rājansañjātavepathuḥsammīlya mṛgaśāvākṣī netre āsītsuvismitā
translation:
Perceiving suddenly the whole of the universe (in his mouth) she was seized with tremor. The lady with eyes resembling those of fawns, closed her eyes, and stayed greatly astonished.
[back to top]
